methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
SwarmFarm Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised AU$30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C). This sample reflects the broader frontier-tech robotics landscape, which includes eight private comparables and one public listing (277810.KQ).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the four peers is $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.8B. This method anchors the analysis to direct market comparables operating in adjacent stages and identical sector classification.

The triangulated range supports a valuation of $1.4B to $1.9B, with a median of $1.6B. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size (n=4) and stage mixing, which introduces variance between Series B and Series C peers. SwarmFarm's Australian domicile and specific technology differentiation relative to the peer set also present material valuation drivers not fully captured by peer median alone. Additional quantitative methods—revenue multiple analysis or DCF modeling—would strengthen conviction but lack requisite data.
